name: SendGrid API
what_it_does: Twilio's cloud-based email delivery platform for sending transactional and marketing emails at scale with delivery tracking, templates, and analytics.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails (receipts, confirmations, password resets) from agents
Email automation workflows triggered by agent actions
Bulk email sending with template management
Email delivery tracking and open/click analytics
Dynamic email content generation with Handlebars templates
not_for: SMS or voice communications (use Twilio instead)
Teams needing completely free high-volume sending
best_when: An agent needs to send transactional emails reliably at scale with good deliverability and tracking.
avoid_when: You only need to send a handful of emails or need end-to-end encryption.

auth.notes: API key authentication with granular permission scopes. Keys can be scoped to specific actions (send only, full access, etc.). Very agent-friendly.

pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials from $19.95/month for 50k emails, Pro from $89.95/month
pricing.notes: Free tier is genuinely useful for development and low-volume agents. Paid tiers unlock more features like dedicated IPs and advanced analytics.

performance.rate_limits: 600 requests/minute on free, higher on paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns structured JSON errors with helpful messages. Error codes are well-documented with fix guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Each API call sends a new email. Agent must track sent state externally to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Domain authentication required for good deliverability - unauthenticated sends go to spam
No idempotency keys - retrying on failure can send duplicate emails
Free tier 100/day limit can be hit quickly in testing
Unsubscribe management required by law - agents must respect suppression lists
Template IDs must be pre-created in dashboard, not via API in free tier
